title: Houstonia ouachitana
taxonomy: Houstonia ouachitana is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the Ouachita Mountains in the United States. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, it is a part of the Rubiales order and is classified under the clade Asterids. The plant's classification is further refined under the kingdom Plantae, and its scientific name follows the binomial nomenclature system. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Houstonia ouachitana, also known as Ouachita bluet, is a perennial wildflower native to the Ouachita Mountains of Arkansas. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Houstonia ouachitana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Houstonia ouachitana is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate coughs, colds, and flu-like symptoms, as well as to boost the immune system.
